item.title: Epic Raises Fortnite V-Bucks Price Amid Rising Costs
item.description: Epic Games has raised the price of Fortnite's V-Bucks in-game currency, citing rising costs as the reason. The price increase will impact fans of the popular game and has sparked a backlash from players.

item.title: UK Warship Departs for Eastern Mediterranean Amid Tensions
item.description: HMS Dragon has departed the UK for the eastern Mediterranean to join the UK's defensive operations in the region, amid tensions with Iran and the Middle East.

item.title: Nottingham Triple Killer Assaulted Husband and Wife Weeks Before Attacks
item.description: A public inquiry into the deaths of three people in Nottingham has heard that the triple killer, Valdo Calocane, assaulted two colleagues weeks before the attacks.

item.title: HMS Dragon Deploys to Cyprus Amid Tensions in the Mediterranean
item.description: The Royal Navy's HMS Dragon has set sail from Portsmouth and is en route to Cyprus, where it will be deployed to the Mediterranean region in response to recent tensions.

item.title: Italy Acquires Rare Caravaggio Portrait for €30 Million
item.description: The Italian state has purchased a rare Caravaggio painting for €30 million, part of a plan to prevent major artworks from being bought by private collectors.

item.title: Neil Simpson and Guide Rob Poth Win Alpine Combined Silver at Winter Paralympics
item.description: Neil Simpson and guide Rob Poth have won Great Britain's first medal at the Winter Paralympics with a silver in the alpine combined. They have two further medal chances later in the Games.

item.title: Government Appoints Donna Ockenden as Leeds Hospitals Trust Maternity Inquiry Chair
item.description: The UK government has appointed Donna Ockenden as the chair of an independent inquiry into maternity failures at Leeds Teaching Hospitals NHS Trust, following a campaign by bereaved and harmed families.

item.title: Signal Warns of Scams as Hackers Target Officials and Users
item.description: Signal has warned its users to be cautious of scams after hackers targeted high-profile users, including officials and journalists, using sophisticated phishing campaigns.

item.title: Inmate Charged with Murder of Notorious Killer Ian Huntley
item.description: A prison inmate has been charged with the murder of Ian Huntley, the former school caretaker who murdered 10-year-olds Holly Wells and Jessica Chapman in 2002.

item.title: Family of Tumbler Ridge Shooting Victim Sues OpenAI
item.description: The family of Maya Gebala, a 12-year-old girl injured in a Canadian school shooting, has sued OpenAI, alleging the company failed to alert authorities to the suspect's plans.

item.title: Violence Against Women: A Growing Epidemic in the UK
item.description: A recent investigation has found that at least 90 women or girls were killed by men in the UK in 2025, with 24 resulting in convictions for murder and six for manslaughter.

item.title: GPS Jamming Disrupts Maritime Navigation in Middle East
item.description: GPS jamming is disrupting maritime navigation in the Middle East, causing significant concern for safety and potentially deadly accidents. Military analysts suspect Iran of being behind the jamming.

item.title: Anthropic Sues US Government Over 'Supply Chain Risk' Label
item.description: Anthropic sues US government over 'supply chain risk' label, citing 'unprecedented and unlawful' action.

item.title: London Offers Alternative to US for Embattled AI Firm Anthropic
item.description: London Mayor Sir Sadiq Khan invites AI firm Anthropic to expand its presence in the city, amidst its deteriorating relationship with the US government.

item.title: Sleep-Enhancing Snacks Gain Popularity Amid Rising Demand for Better Rest
item.description: A mushroom-infused chocolate brand has gained popularity with its sleep-enhancing product, Nightcap, which boasts a list of ingredients that claim to improve sleep quality. The demand for sleep-enhancing products is on the rise, with natural supplements like melatonin being the most popular choice.

item.title: TfL Hack in 2024 Exposed Data of 10 Million People, BBC Investigation Reveals
item.description: A 2024 hack on Transport for London exposed the personal data of around 10 million people, the BBC has discovered, making it one of the biggest hacks in British history.

item.title: Musk Defends Social Media Posts in California Trial
item.description: Elon Musk is defending himself against claims from investors who accuse him of trying to manipulate markets with misleading social media posts. Musk says his posts were 'extremely literal' and not intended to affect the price he would pay for Twitter.

item.title: Online Privacy: A Luxury, Not a Right, in the Digital Age
item.description: Experts warn that online privacy is a luxury, not a right, in the digital age, with many people accepting data use as currency for services and self-censoring due to fear of surveillance.
